api_based_cve_research.overview: Practical guide for using APIs to research CVEs instead of manual browser-based research. This approach is faster, more accurate, and automatable for DevOps workflows. IBM Concert API must be used first for application-specific exposure and package recommendations, then cross-referenced with authoritative public sources.

step.name: Query IBM Concert API First
step.purpose: Confirm whether the target application and package are affected, and capture Concert's remediation guidance
step.follow_up_commands:
source .env && curl -kX GET \ -H "Authorization: ${CONCERT_API_KEY}" \ -H "InstanceId: ${CONCERT_INSTANCE_ID}" \ "${CONCERT_HOSTNAME}/concert/core/api/v1/applications/{id}/vulnerability_details"
source .env && curl -kX GET \ -H "Authorization: ${CONCERT_API_KEY}" \ -H "InstanceId: ${CONCERT_INSTANCE_ID}" \ "${CONCERT_HOSTNAME}/concert/core/api/v1/software_composition/recommendations?package_name=package-name"

extract.field: Recommended package version or remediation path
Evidence that the package is relevant to the current application

step.note: Concert is mandatory for internal remediation context and recommendation gathering.

step.name: Query NVD API for CVE Details
step.purpose: Get official CVE information from the US government database and validate Concert findings
step.parse_with_jq: # Extract key information curl -X GET "https://services.nvd.nist.gov/rest/json/cves/2.0?cveId=CVE-2023-12345" \ -H "Accept: application/json" | jq '{ cve_id: .vulnerabilities[0].cve.id, description: .vulnerabilities[0].cve.descriptions[0].value, cvss_score: .vulnerabilities[0].cve.metrics.cvssMetricV31[0].cvssData.baseScore, severity: .vulnerabilities[0].cve.metrics.cvssMetricV31[0].cvssData.baseSeverity, cwe: .vulnerabilities[0].cve.weaknesses[0].description[0].value }'
step.rate_limit: 5 requests per 30 seconds without API key

step.name: Query GitHub Advisory Database
step.authentication: Optional: Use GitHub token for higher rate limits

step.name: Query OSV API for Cross-Ecosystem Data
step.purpose: Get vulnerability data across multiple ecosystems
step.parse_with_jq:
# Extract vulnerability details and filter by CVE ID curl -X POST "https://api.osv.dev/v1/query" \ -H "Content-Type: application/json" \ -d '{"package":{"name":"flask","ecosystem":"PyPI"},"version":"2.3.0"}' | \ jq '.vulns[] | select(.aliases[]? | contains("CVE-2023-30861")) | { id: .id, aliases: .aliases, summary: .summary }'
# Or get details of a specific vulnerability by ID curl -X GET "https://api.osv.dev/v1/vulns/PYSEC-2023-62" | jq '{ id: .id, aliases: .aliases, summary: .summary, affected: .affected[0].ranges[0] }'
step.note: OSV API returns all vulnerabilities for a package/version. Filter results by CVE ID using jq or query specific vulnerability IDs directly.

step.name: Validate Concert Against External Sources
step.purpose: Ensure consistency and accuracy before choosing the remediation version

validation_checklist.item: ✅ Concert confirms the affected application/package relationship
✅ NVD confirms the same CVE and severity context
✅ GitHub Advisory and/or OSV confirm affected version ranges
✅ Concert recommendation is compared against patched versions from public sources
✅ Any discrepancy is documented explicitly in the remediation notes or PR

example.name: python_flask_vulnerability
example.commands:
# Step 1: NVD API curl -X GET "https://services.nvd.nist.gov/rest/json/cves/2.0?cveId=CVE-2023-30861" \ -H "Accept: application/json" | jq '.vulnerabilities[0].cve | { description: .descriptions[0].value, cvss: .metrics.cvssMetricV31[0].cvssData.baseScore, severity: .metrics.cvssMetricV31[0].cvssData.baseSeverity }'
# Step 2: GitHub Advisory curl -X GET "https://api.github.com/advisories?cve_id=CVE-2023-30861" \ -H "Accept: application/vnd.github+json" | jq '.[0] | { package: .vulnerabilities[0].package.name, vulnerable: .vulnerabilities[0].vulnerable_version_range, patched: .vulnerabilities[0].patched_versions }'
# Step 3: OSV API curl -X POST "https://api.osv.dev/v1/query" \ -H "Content-Type: application/json" \ -d '{"package":{"name":"flask","ecosystem":"PyPI"},"version":"2.2.0"}' | jq '.vulns[] | select(.id | contains("CVE-2023-30861"))'

expected_output.osv: Confirms same version ranges and fix versions

example.name: nodejs_express_vulnerability
example.commands:
# Combined query with jq filtering CVE_ID="CVE-2024-29041"
# NVD curl -s "https://services.nvd.nist.gov/rest/json/cves/2.0?cveId=${CVE_ID}" | \ jq -r '.vulnerabilities[0].cve.descriptions[0].value'
# GitHub Advisory curl -s "https://api.github.com/advisories?cve_id=${CVE_ID}" \ -H "Accept: application/vnd.github+json" | \ jq '.[0].vulnerabilities[0] | "Package: \(.package.name), Vulnerable: \(.vulnerable_version_range), Fixed: \(.patched_versions)"'

api_response_documentation_template.title: Document API Research Results

tip.title: Create a Shell Script for Repeated Research
tip.script:
#!/bin/bash # cve_research.sh - Automated CVE research using APIs
CVE_ID=$1
if [ -z "$CVE_ID" ]; then echo "Usage: ./cve_research.sh CVE-YYYY-XXXXX" exit 1 fi
echo "=== Researching $CVE_ID ===" echo
echo "1. NVD API:" curl -s "https://services.nvd.nist.gov/rest/json/cves/2.0?cveId=${CVE_ID}" | \ jq '.vulnerabilities[0].cve | { description: .descriptions[0].value, cvss: .metrics.cvssMetricV31[0].cvssData.baseScore, severity: .metrics.cvssMetricV31[0].cvssData.baseSeverity }'
echo echo "2. GitHub Advisory:" curl -s "https://api.github.com/advisories?cve_id=${CVE_ID}" \ -H "Accept: application/vnd.github+json" | \ jq '.[0] | { package: .vulnerabilities[0].package.name, vulnerable: .vulnerabilities[0].vulnerable_version_range, patched: .vulnerabilities[0].patched_versions }'

tip.title: Use Environment Variables for API Keys
tip.example:
# For GitHub API with authentication export GITHUB_TOKEN="your_token_here"
curl -X GET "https://api.github.com/advisories?cve_id=CVE-2023-12345" \ -H "Authorization: Bearer ${GITHUB_TOKEN}" \ -H "Accept: application/vnd.github+json"

tip.title: Cache API Responses to Avoid Rate Limits
tip.example:
# Cache NVD response CVE_ID="CVE-2023-12345" CACHE_FILE="/tmp/nvd_${CVE_ID}.json"
if [ ! -f "$CACHE_FILE" ]; then curl -s "https://services.nvd.nist.gov/rest/json/cves/2.0?cveId=${CVE_ID}" > "$CACHE_FILE" fi
jq '.vulnerabilities[0].cve' "$CACHE_FILE"

error.solution: Wait 30 seconds between requests, or use API key for higher limits
error.workaround: Use cached responses or switch to GitHub Advisory API

error.solution: Verify CVE ID format (CVE-YYYY-XXXXX), check if CVE is recently published
error.workaround: Try alternative APIs (OSV, MITRE) or wait for database sync
Use alternative API sources or fallback to manual research

best_practices.practice: Always query multiple APIs to cross-validate information
Use jq to parse JSON responses for clean, structured output
Cache API responses to avoid hitting rate limits
Create reusable scripts for common CVE research patterns
Document all API queries and responses in PR descriptions
